Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 88 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 2.8x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 88 actions returned the board to a state already seen 88 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 88 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

Wait, looking at the image, the CHARCOAL shape is now on the RIGHT side and the yellow shape is on the LEFT. It seems like the panels swapped! The game is over because I converted the wrong object. Let me think about this differently.
Looking at the current image:
- LEFT panel: black shape with white dots (was yellow)
- RIGHT panel: CHARCOAL shape (was charcoal)
- BOTTOM RIGHT: yellow shape
So SPACE was converting the yellow shape to black, but this caused a game over. The goal was probably NOT to convert yellow to black.
Let me re-examine. The game is over. I need to restart or figure out what the actual goal was. Since game_over is True, I should probably just accept this and move on. But wait - there's no "run_complete" key to restart. Let me check if there's a way to continue.
Actually, looking more carefully, the game is over but not run_complete. Maybe I need to try a different approach. Let me see if I can still take actions.
